name: Steve Coogan
photo_credit: Gordon Correll via Wikimedia Commons (CC BY-SA 2.0)
blurb: The character actor's character actor — an impressionist who workshopped a fictional sports presenter named Alan Partridge on stage until the persona swallowed the man.
bio: Steve Coogan came up through the Manchester Polytechnic drama school and the British stand-up circuit of the late 1980s as a virtuoso impressionist — Spitting Image voiced him for years before audiences knew his face — and won the 1992 Edinburgh Perrier Award for a two-hander with John Thomson built on character sketches. On stage he hatched the working-class Mancunian wastrel Paul Calf and his drag-sister Pauline, then, on Radio 4's On the Hour with Armando Iannucci, the inept sports-presenter Alan Partridge, a creation that would migrate from radio to television (Knowing Me, Knowing You; I'm Alan Partridge) to feature film (Alpha Papa, 2013) and become one of the most-cited cringe-comedy templates in British TV. Coogan returned to live stand-up only sporadically after the late 1990s; the character work he built in front of small rooms in Ipswich and Edinburgh is the foundation everything else stands on.

timeline.ev: Born October 14 in Middleton, Lancashire, England
Voice work begins on Spitting Image; touring stand-up as an impressionist
Co-creates Alan Partridge with Armando Iannucci on BBC Radio 4's On the Hour
Wins the Perrier Award at Edinburgh Festival Fringe (with John Thomson)
Knowing Me, Knowing You with Alan Partridge — TV; The Day Today airs
I'm Alan Partridge debuts on BBC; wins two BAFTAs
Co-founds Baby Cow Productions with Henry Normal
First stand-up tour in a decade: 'Steve Coogan as Alan Partridge and other less successful characters'
Alan Partridge: Alpha Papa (feature film); Philomena (Oscar nom for Best Adapted Screenplay)

accolades: Perrier Award winner, Edinburgh Festival Fringe (1992)
Six BAFTA Awards (including Best Adapted Screenplay for Philomena)
Two Academy Award nominations (Best Picture and Best Adapted Screenplay for Philomena)
Alan Partridge voted #7 on Channel 4's 100 Greatest TV Characters (2001)

appearances.t: Steve Coogan as Alan Partridge and Other Less Successful Characters
The Man Who Thinks He's It (live tour)
